---
date: 2021-03-18
draft: false
image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1489945052260-4f21c52268b9?crop=entropy&cs=tinysrgb&fit=max&fm=jpg&ixid=MnwxMTc3M3wwfDF8c2VhcmNofDI1fHxoZWxsb3xlbnwwfHx8fDE2MTY2OTkzOTk&ixlib=rb-1.2.1&q=80&w=2000"
---

# Welcome to Julia for Data Science

Julia for Data Science started in 2021 as a newsletter hosted on Ghost — a subscription platform for independent publishers. The goal was simple: high-quality articles to help people learn Julia with an emphasis on data science, covering topics like performance tips, package tours, and hands-on tutorials.

## A New Home

In 2025, the site moved from Ghost to [Quarto](https://quarto.org), an open-source scientific publishing system. The motivations were straightforward:

- **No vendor lock-in** — content lives in plain Markdown files under version control
- **Free to host** — deployed to GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions
- **Better for technical content** — native support for executable Julia code, LaTeX math, and syntax highlighting

All posts from the Ghost era have been preserved and are still available here.

## Stay Updated

The newsletter is no more, but you can follow along via the [RSS feed](/index.xml) — subscribe in your feed reader of choice to get notified when new posts go up.

You can also reach out on Twitter: [@JuliaForDataSci](https://twitter.com/JuliaForDataSci)
